Paragraph

Suppose that \(f\) is a bounded function defined on the interval \([a, b]\text{.}\) Then its upper integral, and respectively lower integral, on \([a, b]\) with respect to \(\alpha\) is defined as \(\inf_{\cP}U(f, \cP, \alpha)\text{,}\) and respectively \(\sup_{\cP}U(f, \cP, \alpha)\text{,}\) where \(\cP\) runs over all partitions of \(\cP\text{.}\)
in-context
